</div></div></th></tr><tr><td colspan="2" class="infobox-full-data"><div style="text-align: right">Source: <a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="http://www.cricinfo.com/india/content/player/28081.html">ESPNcricinfo</a>, <span class="nowrap">30 March 2025</span></div></td></tr><tr style="display:none"><td colspan="2">
</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2" class="infobox-below" style="border-top: 1px solid right;"><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
<p><b>Mahendra Singh  Dhoni</b> (<span class="IPA nowrap" lang="hi-Latn-fonipa"><a href="/wiki/Help:IPA/Hindi_and_Urdu" title="Help:IPA/Hindi and Urdu">[məˈɦeːnd̪ɾə<span class="wrap"> </span>ˈsɪŋɡʱ<span class="wrap"> </span>ˈd̪ʱoːniː]</a></span> <span class="ext-phonos"><span data-nosnippet="" id="ooui-php-1" class="noexcerpt ext-phonos-PhonosButton ext-phonos-PhonosButton-emptylabel oo-ui-widget oo-ui-widget-enabled oo-ui-buttonElement oo-ui-buttonElement-frameless oo-ui-iconElement oo-ui-buttonWidget" data-ooui="{"_":"mw.Phonos.PhonosButton","href":"\/\/upload.wikimedia.org\/wikipedia\/commons\/transcoded\/a\/a4\/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni.ogg\/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni.ogg.mp3","rel":["nofollow"],"framed":false,"icon":"volumeUp","data":{"ipa":"","text":"","lang":"en","wikibase":"","file":"Mahendra Singh Dhoni.ogg"},"classes":["noexcerpt","ext-phonos-PhonosButton","ext-phonos-PhonosButton-emptylabel"]}"><a role="button" tabindex="0" href="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/a/a4/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni.ogg/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni.ogg.mp3" rel="nofollow" aria-label="Play audio" title="Play audio" class="oo-ui-buttonElement-button"><span class="oo-ui-iconElement-icon oo-ui-icon-volumeUp"></span><span class="oo-ui-labelElement-label"></span><span class="oo-ui-indicatorElement-indicator oo-ui-indicatorElement-noIndicator"></span></a></span><sup class="ext-phonos-attribution noexcerpt navigation-not-searchable"><a href="/wiki/File:Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni.ogg" title="File:Mahendra Singh Dhoni.ogg">ⓘ</a></sup></span>; born 7 July 1981) is an Indian professional <a href="/wiki/Cricketer" class="mw-redirect" title="Cricketer">cricketer</a> who plays as a right-handed <a href="/wiki/Batsman" class="mw-redirect" title="Batsman">batter</a> and a <a href="/wiki/Wicket-keeper" title="Wicket-keeper">wicket-keeper</a>. Widely regarded as one of the most prolific wicket-keeper batsmen and <a href="/wiki/Captain_(cricket)" title="Captain (cricket)">captains</a>, he represented the <a href="/wiki/Indian_national_cricket_team" class="mw-redirect" title="Indian national cricket team">Indian cricket team</a> and was the captain of the side in <a href="/wiki/Limited_overs_cricket" title="Limited overs cricket">limited overs</a> formats from 2007 to 2017 and in <a href="/wiki/Test_cricket" title="Test cricket">Test cricket</a> from 2008 to 2014. Dhoni has captained the most international matches and is the most successful Indian captain. He has led India to victory in the <a href="/wiki/2007_ICC_World_Twenty20" class="mw-redirect" title="2007 ICC World Twenty20">2007 ICC World Twenty20</a>, the <a href="/wiki/2011_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2011 Cricket World Cup">2011 Cricket World Cup</a>, and the <a href="/wiki/2013_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2013 ICC Champions Trophy">2013 ICC Champions Trophy</a>, being the only captain to win three different limited overs ICC tournaments. He also led the teams that won the <a href="/wiki/Asia_Cup" title="Asia Cup">Asia Cup</a> in <a href="/wiki/2010_Asia_Cup" title="2010 Asia Cup">2010</a> and <a href="/wiki/2016_Asia_Cup" title="2016 Asia Cup">2016</a>, and he was a member of the title winning squad in <a href="/wiki/2018_Asia_Cup" title="2018 Asia Cup">2018</a>.
</p><p>Born in <a href="/wiki/Ranchi" title="Ranchi">Ranchi</a>, Dhoni made his <a href="/wiki/First_class_cricket" class="mw-redirect" title="First class cricket">first class</a> debut for <a href="/wiki/Bihar_cricket_team" title="Bihar cricket team">Bihar</a> in 1999. He made his debut for the Indian cricket team on 23 December 2004 in an <a href="/wiki/One_day_international" class="mw-redirect" title="One day international">ODI</a> against <a href="/wiki/Bangladesh_national_cricket_team" title="Bangladesh national cricket team">Bangladesh</a> and played his first test a year later against <a href="/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_cricket_team" title="Sri Lanka national cricket team">Sri Lanka</a>. In 2007, he became the captain of the ODI side before taking over in all formats by 2008. Dhoni retired from Test cricket in 2014 but continued playing in limited overs cricket till 2019. He has scored 17,266 runs in <a href="/wiki/International_cricket" title="International cricket">international cricket</a> including 10,000 plus runs at an <a href="/wiki/Batting_average_(cricket)" title="Batting average (cricket)">average</a> of more than 50 in ODIs.
</p><p>In the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Premier_League" title="Indian Premier League">Indian Premier League</a> (IPL), Dhoni plays for <a href="/wiki/Chennai_Super_Kings" title="Chennai Super Kings">Chennai Super Kings</a> (CSK), leading them to the final on ten occasions and winning it five times (<a href="/wiki/2010_Indian_Premier_League" title="2010 Indian Premier League">2010</a>, <a href="/wiki/2011_Indian_Premier_League" title="2011 Indian Premier League">2011</a>, <a href="/wiki/2018_Indian_Premier_League" title="2018 Indian Premier League">2018</a>, <a href="/wiki/2021_Indian_Premier_League" title="2021 Indian Premier League">2021</a> and <a href="/wiki/2023_Indian_Premier_League" title="2023 Indian Premier League">2023</a>) jointly sharing this title with <a href="/wiki/Rohit_Sharma" title="Rohit Sharma">Rohit Sharma</a> . He has also led CSK to two <a href="/wiki/Champions_League_T20" class="mw-redirect" title="Champions League T20">Champions League T20</a> titles in <a href="/wiki/2010_Champions_League_Twenty20" title="2010 Champions League Twenty20">2010</a> and <a href="/wiki/2014_Champions_League_Twenty20" title="2014 Champions League Twenty20">2014</a>. Dhoni is among the few batsmen to have scored more than five thousand runs in the IPL, as well as being the first <a href="/wiki/Wicket-keeper" title="Wicket-keeper">wicket-keeper</a> to do so.
</p><p>In 2008, Dhoni was awarded India's highest sport honour <a href="/wiki/Khel_Ratna_Award" title="Khel Ratna Award">Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award</a> by <a href="/wiki/Government_of_India" title="Government of India">Government of India</a>. He received the fourth highest civilian award <a href="/wiki/Padma_Shri" title="Padma Shri">Padma Shri</a> in 2009 and third highest civilian award <a href="/wiki/Padma_Bhushan" title="Padma Bhushan">Padma Bhushan</a> in 2018. Dhoni holds an <a href="/wiki/Honorary_rank" class="mw-redirect" title="Honorary rank">honorary rank</a> of <a href="/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel" title="Lieutenant colonel">Lieutenant colonel</a> in the <a href="/wiki/Parachute_Regiment_(India)" title="Parachute Regiment (India)">Parachute Regiment</a> of the <a href="/wiki/Territorial_Army_(India)" title="Territorial Army (India)">Indian Territorial Army</a> which was presented to him by the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Army" title="Indian Army">Indian Army</a> in 2011. In June 2025, he was inducted into <a href="/wiki/ICC_Cricket_Hall_of_Fame" title="ICC Cricket Hall of Fame">ICC Cricket Hall of Fame</a>.
</p>
<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc">
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Early_life">Early life</h2></div>
<p>Dhoni was born on 7 July 1981 in <a href="/wiki/Ranchi" title="Ranchi">Ranchi</a>, Bihar (now in <a href="/wiki/Jharkhand" title="Jharkhand">Jharkhand</a>) in a Hindu <a href="/wiki/Rajput" title="Rajput">Rajput</a> family to Pan Singh and Devaki Devi.<sup id="cite_ref-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-3" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> His parents hailed from Lwali village in the <a href="/wiki/Almora_district" title="Almora district">Almora district</a> of <a href="/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh" title="Uttar Pradesh">Uttar Pradesh</a> (now <a href="/wiki/Uttarakhand" title="Uttarakhand">Uttarakhand</a>). He was the youngest of three children.<sup id="cite_ref-Sandhu_4-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Sandhu-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-5" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-5"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-6" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-6"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> His family spells the surname as "Dhauni".<sup id="cite_ref-7" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-7"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The spelling "Dhoni" emerged due to a spelling mistake in his school certificates and, despite repeated attempts by his family, has never been rectified.<sup id="cite_ref-8" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-8"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni did his schooling from DAV Jawahar Vidya Mandir, where he started playing <a href="/wiki/Association_football" title="Association football">football</a> as a <a href="/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football)" title="Goalkeeper (association football)">goalkeeper</a>, but later moved to play cricket on the suggestion of his coach Keshav Banerjee.<sup id="cite_ref-9" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-9"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-10" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-10"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> From 2001 to 2003, Dhoni worked as a <a href="/wiki/Conductor_(rail)" title="Conductor (rail)">Travelling Ticket Examiner</a> (TTE) at <a href="/wiki/Kharagpur_railway_station" class="mw-redirect" title="Kharagpur railway station">Kharagpur</a> under <a href="/wiki/South_Eastern_Railway_zone" title="South Eastern Railway zone">South Eastern Railway zone</a> of <a href="/wiki/Indian_Railways" title="Indian Railways">Indian Railways</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-11" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-11"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>11<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-12" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-12"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>12<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Youth_career">Youth career</h2></div>
<p>He played as a <a href="/wiki/Wicket-keeper" title="Wicket-keeper">wicket-keeper</a> for Commando cricket club from 1995 to 1998 and <a href="/wiki/Central_Coalfields_Limited" class="mw-redirect" title="Central Coalfields Limited">Central Coal Fields Limited</a> (CCL) team in 1998.<sup id="cite_ref-13" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-13"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>13<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> At CCL, he batted higher up the order and helped the team qualify to the higher division.<sup id="cite_ref-14" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-14"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>14<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Based on his performance at club cricket, he was picked for the 1997/98 season of <a href="/wiki/Vinoo_Mankad_Trophy" title="Vinoo Mankad Trophy">Vinoo Mankad Trophy</a> under-16 championship.<sup id="cite_ref-15" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-15"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>15<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-16" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-16"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>16<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the 1998–99, Dhoni played for Bihar U-19 team in the <a href="/wiki/Cooch_Behar_Trophy" title="Cooch Behar Trophy">Cooch Behar Trophy</a> and scored 176 runs in 5 matches. In the 1999–2000 Cooch Behar Trophy, the Bihar U-19 cricket team made it to the finals, where Dhoni made 84 in a losing cause.<sup id="cite_ref-17" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-17"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>17<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni's contribution in the tournament included 488 runs in nine matches with five fifties, 17 catches and seven <a href="/wiki/Stumping" class="mw-redirect" title="Stumping">stumpings</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-18" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-18"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>18<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni made it to the East Zone U-19 squad for the <a href="/wiki/C._K._Nayudu_Trophy" title="C. K. Nayudu Trophy">C. K. Nayudu Trophy</a> in the 1999–2000 season and scored only 97 runs in four matches, as East Zone lost all the matches and finished last in the tournament.<sup id="cite_ref-19" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-19"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>19<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-20" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-20"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>20<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni made his <a href="/wiki/Ranji_Trophy" title="Ranji Trophy">Ranji Trophy</a> debut for <a href="/wiki/Bihar_cricket_team" title="Bihar cricket team">Bihar</a> against <a href="/wiki/Assam_cricket_team" title="Assam cricket team">Assam</a> in the 1999–2000 season, as an eighteen-year-old scoring 68 runs in the second innings.<sup id="cite_ref-21" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-21"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>21<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni finished the season with 283 runs in 5 matches. Dhoni scored his maiden first-class century while playing for Bihar against <a href="/wiki/Bengal_cricket_team" title="Bengal cricket team">Bengal</a> in the <a href="/wiki/2000%E2%80%9301_Ranji_Trophy" title="2000–01 Ranji Trophy">2000–01 Ranji Trophy</a> season.<sup id="cite_ref-22" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-22"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>22<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Apart from this century, his performance in the 2000/01 season did not include another score over fifty and in the <a href="/wiki/2001%E2%80%9302_Ranji_Trophy" title="2001–02 Ranji Trophy">2001–02 Ranji Trophy</a> season, he scored just five fifties in four Ranji matches.<sup id="cite_ref-23" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-23"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>23<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-24" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-24"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>24<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni played for <a href="/wiki/Jharkhand_cricket_team" title="Jharkhand cricket team">Jharkhand</a> in the <a href="/wiki/2002%E2%80%9303_Ranji_Trophy" title="2002–03 Ranji Trophy">2002–03 Ranji Trophy</a> and represented <a href="/wiki/East_Zone_cricket_team" title="East Zone cricket team">East Zone</a> in the <a href="/wiki/Deodhar_Trophy" title="Deodhar Trophy">Deodhar Trophy</a> where he started gaining recognition for his lower-order contribution as well as hard-hitting batting style. In the 2003/04 season, Dhoni scored a century (128*) against Assam in the first match of the <a href="/wiki/Vijay_Hazare_Trophy" title="Vijay Hazare Trophy">Ranji ODI</a> tournament and was part of the <a href="/wiki/East_Zone_cricket_team" title="East Zone cricket team">East Zone</a> squad that won the <a href="/wiki/Deodhar_Trophy" title="Deodhar Trophy">Deodhar Trophy</a>, scoring 244 runs in four matches.<sup id="cite_ref-25" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-25"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>25<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-26" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-26"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>26<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>In the <a href="/wiki/Duleep_Trophy" title="Duleep Trophy">Duleep Trophy</a> finals, Dhoni represented East Zone and scored a fighting half-century in the second innings in a losing cause.<sup id="cite_ref-27" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-27"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>27<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was identified as one of the emerging talents via the <a href="/wiki/Board_of_Control_for_Cricket_in_India" title="Board of Control for Cricket in India">BCCI</a>'s small-town talent-spotting initiative <a href="/wiki/Talent_Resource_Development_Wing" title="Talent Resource Development Wing">TRDW</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-28" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-28"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>28<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-29" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-29"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>29<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In 2004, Dhoni was picked for the <a href="/wiki/India_A" class="mw-redirect" title="India A">India A</a> squad for a tour of <a href="/wiki/Zimbabwe_national_cricket_team" title="Zimbabwe national cricket team">Zimbabwe</a> and <a href="/wiki/Kenya_national_cricket_team" title="Kenya national cricket team">Kenya</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-30" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-30"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>30<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Against the Zimbabwe XI in <a href="/wiki/Harare_Sports_Club" title="Harare Sports Club">Harare Sports Club</a>, Dhoni contributed seven catches and four stumpings.<sup id="cite_ref-31" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-31"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>31<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the tri-nation tournament involving Kenya, India A and Pakistan A, Dhoni helped India A chase down their target of 223 against Pakistan A with a half-century and scored 362 runs in six innings at an average of 72.40 with back to back centuries.<sup id="cite_ref-32" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-32"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>32<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-33" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-33"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>33<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-34" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-34"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>34<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="International_career">International career</h2></div>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Debut_and_early_years">Debut and early years</h3></div>
<p>The Indian ODI team in the early 2000s saw <a href="/wiki/Rahul_Dravid" title="Rahul Dravid">Rahul Dravid</a> as the wicket-keeper to ensure that the wicket-keeper spot didn't lack in batting talent and also tried other wicket-keeper/batsmen like <a href="/wiki/Parthiv_Patel" title="Parthiv Patel">Parthiv Patel</a> and <a href="/wiki/Dinesh_Karthik" title="Dinesh Karthik">Dinesh Karthik</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-35" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-35"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>35<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> With Dhoni performing well for the India A squad, he was picked in the ODI squad for the <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Bangladesh_in_2004-05" class="mw-redirect" title="Indian cricket team in Bangladesh in 2004-05">Bangladesh tour</a> in December 2004.<sup id="cite_ref-36" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-36"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>36<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni made his debut in the first match of the series and was <a href="/wiki/Run_out" title="Run out">run out</a> for a <a href="/wiki/Duck_(cricket)" title="Duck (cricket)">duck</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-37" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-37"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>37<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was picked for the subsequent <a href="/wiki/Pakistani_cricket_team_in_India_in_2004-05" class="mw-redirect" title="Pakistani cricket team in India in 2004-05">ODI series</a> against <a href="/wiki/Pakistan_national_cricket_team" title="Pakistan national cricket team">Pakistan</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-38" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-38"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>38<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the second match of the series in <a href="/wiki/ACA-VDCA_Stadium" class="mw-redirect" title="ACA-VDCA Stadium">Visakhapatnam</a>, Dhoni playing in his fifth one-day international, scored 148 runs off 123 deliveries which surpassed the earlier record for the highest score by an Indian wicket-keeper.<sup id="cite_ref-39" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-39"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>39<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni played in the <a href="/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cricket_team_in_India_in_2005-06" class="mw-redirect" title="Sri Lankan cricket team in India in 2005-06">Sri Lankan bilateral ODI series</a> in October–November 2005 and was promoted to No. 3 in the batting order in the third ODI at <a href="/wiki/Sawai_Mansingh_Stadium" title="Sawai Mansingh Stadium">Jaipur</a> where he scored an unbeaten 183 runs off 145 balls, winning the game for India.<sup id="cite_ref-40" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-40"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>40<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The innings would surpass his earlier record for the highest score by an Indian wicket-keeper and was described in <a href="/wiki/Wisden_Almanack" class="mw-redirect" title="Wisden Almanack">Wisden Almanack</a> as 'Uninhibited, yet anything but crude'.<sup id="cite_ref-41" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-41"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>41<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> It was also the highest individual score in ODI cricket in a run chase, a record which was broken seven years later by <a href="/wiki/Shane_Watson" title="Shane Watson">Shane Watson</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-42" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-42"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>42<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-43" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-43"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>43<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni ended the series with the highest aggregate of 346 runs and was awarded the <a href="/wiki/Player_of_the_Match_awards_(cricket)" title="Player of the Match awards (cricket)">Man of the series</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-44" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-44"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>44<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<figure class="mw-default-size mw-halign-left" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:Dhoni_behind_Stumps.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/Dhoni_behind_Stumps.jpg/250px-Dhoni_behind_Stumps.jpg" decoding="async" width="250" height="199" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/Dhoni_behind_Stumps.jpg/500px-Dhoni_behind_Stumps.jpg 1.5x" data-file-width="761" data-file-height="606"></a><figcaption>Dhoni <a href="/wiki/Wicket-keeping" class="mw-redirect" title="Wicket-keeping">keeping wickets</a> in a test match</figcaption></figure>
<p>Dhoni became a regular in the Indian side after the <a href="/wiki/South_African_cricket_team_in_India_in_2005%E2%80%9306" title="South African cricket team in India in 2005–06">home series</a> against <a href="/wiki/South_Africa_national_cricket_team" title="South Africa national cricket team">South Africa</a> in November 2005.<sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In December 2005, Dhoni was awarded a "B" grade contract by the BCCI.<sup id="cite_ref-46" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-46"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>46<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni made his test debut in the same month against Sri Lanka during their <a href="/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cricket_team_in_India_in_2005%E2%80%9306" title="Sri Lankan cricket team in India in 2005–06">tour of India</a> at <a href="/wiki/M._A._Chidambaram_Stadium" title="M. A. Chidambaram Stadium">Chennai</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-47" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-47"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>47<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-48" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-48"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>48<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored 30 runs in his debut match, that was marred by rain and ended in a draw.<sup id="cite_ref-49" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-49"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>49<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni made his maiden half-century in the second test leading to an Indian win.<sup id="cite_ref-50" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-50"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>50<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni played all the matches in the subsequent <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Pakistan_in_2005%E2%80%9306" title="Indian cricket team in Pakistan in 2005–06">tour of Pakistan</a> scoring 219 runs in five ODI matches and 179 runs in five tests including his maiden test century in the second test in <a href="/wiki/Iqbal_Stadium" title="Iqbal Stadium">Faisalabad</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Test_51-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Test-51"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>51<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He scored 106 runs across three tests in the <a href="/wiki/English_cricket_team_in_India_in_2005%E2%80%9306" title="English cricket team in India in 2005–06">home series</a> against <a href="/wiki/England_cricket_team" title="England cricket team">England</a> in March 2006 and 177 runs in five ODI series that followed.<sup id="cite_ref-Test_51-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Test-51"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>51<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni dropped multiple catches and missed dismissal chances including a key stumping opportunity of <a href="/wiki/Andrew_Flintoff" title="Andrew Flintoff">Andrew Flintoff</a> which led to criticism of his wicket-keeping.<sup id="cite_ref-52" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-52"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>52<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>In the <a href="/wiki/DLF_Cup_2006-07" class="mw-redirect" title="DLF Cup 2006-07">DLF Cup 2006-07</a>, Dhoni scored 43 runs as the team lost twice in three games and did not qualify for the finals. In the <a href="/wiki/2006_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2006 ICC Champions Trophy">2006 ICC Champions Trophy</a>, India lost to <a href="/wiki/West_Indies_cricket_team" title="West Indies cricket team">West Indies</a> and <a href="/wiki/Australia_cricket_team" class="mw-redirect" title="Australia cricket team">Australia</a>, though Dhoni scored a half-century against West Indies and failed to make it to the knock out stage.<sup id="cite_ref-53" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-53"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>53<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_South_Africa_in_2006-07" class="mw-redirect" title="Indian cricket team in South Africa in 2006-07">ODI series</a> in <a href="/wiki/South_African_cricket_team" class="mw-redirect" title="South African cricket team">South Africa</a> in November 2006, Dhoni scored 139 runs in four matches in the series loss.<sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the test series that followed, Dhoni scored 114 runs in two tests including a first test victory in South Africa in the first test, but was ruled out of the third test with injury.<sup id="cite_ref-54" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-54"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>54<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni made his <a href="/wiki/T20_international" class="mw-redirect" title="T20 international">T20 international</a> debut in December 2006 against South Africa at <a href="/wiki/Wanderers_Stadium" title="Wanderers Stadium">Johannesburg</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-55" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-55"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>55<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was subsequently named in the <a href="/wiki/ICC_Men%27s_ODI_Team_of_the_Year" title="ICC Men's ODI Team of the Year">ODI team of the year</a> by the <a href="/wiki/International_Cricket_Council" title="International Cricket Council">ICC</a> for 2006.<sup id="cite_ref-56" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-56"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>56<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="2007_World_T20_and_captaincy">2007 World T20 and captaincy</h3></div>
<p>India recorded identical 3–1 victory over <a href="/wiki/West_Indian_cricket_team_in_India_in_2006-07" class="mw-redirect" title="West Indian cricket team in India in 2006-07">West Indies</a> and <a href="/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cricket_team_in_India_in_2006-07" class="mw-redirect" title="Sri Lankan cricket team in India in 2006-07">Sri Lanka</a> in early 2007 with Dhoni averaging more than 100 in both the series. Subsequently, Dhoni was part of the squad for the <a href="/wiki/2007_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2007 Cricket World Cup">2007 Cricket World Cup</a> in which India unexpectedly crashed out in the group stage after losses to Bangladesh and Sri Lanka with Dhoni scoring ducks in both these matches and just 29 runs in the tournament.<sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-3" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-57" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-57"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>57<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> As a result, Dhoni's house in Ranchi was vandalised and damaged by activists of <a href="/wiki/Jharkhand_Mukti_Morcha" title="Jharkhand Mukti Morcha">JMM</a> and security was tightened for his family.<sup id="cite_ref-58" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-58"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>58<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored 91* against <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Bangladesh_in_2007" title="Indian cricket team in Bangladesh in 2007">Bangladesh</a> in the first match of the ODI series in May 2007 which got him the Man of the Match award while also later winning the Man of the Series award after the third game of the series was washed away.<sup id="cite_ref-59" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-59"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>59<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni played for <a href="/wiki/ACC_Asia_XI_cricket_team" title="ACC Asia XI cricket team">ACC Asia XI cricket team</a> in the <a href="/wiki/Afro-Asia_Cup" title="Afro-Asia Cup">Afro-Asia Cup</a>, scoring 174 runs in three matches at an average of 87 including 139 off 97 balls in the third ODI.<sup id="cite_ref-60" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-60"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>60<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni was named vice-captain of the ODI team for the <a href="/wiki/2007_Future_Cup" title="2007 Future Cup">2007 Future Cup</a> against South Africa in <a href="/wiki/Ireland" title="Ireland">Ireland</a> and the subsequent <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_England_in_2007" title="Indian cricket team in England in 2007">seven-match series</a> against England.<sup id="cite_ref-61" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-61"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>61<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was awarded an 'A' grade contract by BCCI in June 2007.<sup id="cite_ref-62" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-62"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>62<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was appointed as the captain of the Indian squad for the inaugural <a href="/wiki/2007_ICC_World_Twenty20" class="mw-redirect" title="2007 ICC World Twenty20">World Twenty20</a> in September 2007.<sup id="cite_ref-63" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-63"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>63<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led India to victory in the tournament after defeating Pakistan in the final.<sup id="cite_ref-64" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-64"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>64<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-65" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-65"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>65<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni was later appointed as the captain of the Indian cricket team in all formats.<sup id="cite_ref-66" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-66"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>66<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>On 2 September 2007, Dhoni equalled <a href="/wiki/Adam_Gilchrist" title="Adam Gilchrist">Adam Gilchrist</a>'s international record for the most dismissals in an innings in ODI by effecting six dismissals against England.<sup id="cite_ref-67" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-67"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>67<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni took his first and only wicket in international cricket on 30 September 2009 when he bowled <a href="/wiki/Travis_Dowlin" title="Travis Dowlin">Travis Dowlin</a> of West Indies in the <a href="/wiki/2009_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2009 ICC Champions Trophy">2009 ICC Champions Trophy</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-68" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-68"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>68<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> However, he scored just three runs in the only match he batted with India crashing out of the series in the group stage after the match against Australia was washed out.<sup id="cite_ref-69" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-69"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>69<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni averaged more than 60 in the <a href="/wiki/International_cricket_in_2008-09" class="mw-redirect" title="International cricket in 2008-09">2008-09 season</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-70" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-70"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>70<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored two centuries during <a href="/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cricket_team_in_India_in_2009%E2%80%9310" title="Sri Lankan cricket team in India in 2009–10">Sri Lanka's tour of India</a> in November 2009 which India won to achieve the top ranking in ICC test ranking for the first time in its history.<sup id="cite_ref-71" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-71"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>71<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni had an excellent year in ODIs in 2009, scoring 1198 runs in just 24 innings, at an average of 70.43 and topped the ICC ODI batsman rankings for several months.<sup id="cite_ref-72" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-72"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>72<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He was named as the captain and wicket-keeper of the ICC ODI Team of the year.<sup id="cite_ref-73" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-73"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>73<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="2011_World_Cup_win_and_later">2011 World Cup win and later</h3></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:Wicketkeeping_kit_and_bat_of_MS_Dhoni_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum,_Pune.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/67/Wicketkeeping_kit_and_bat_of_MS_Dhoni_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg/250px-Wicketkeeping_kit_and_bat_of_MS_Dhoni_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg" decoding="async" width="250" height="141" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/67/Wicketkeeping_kit_and_bat_of_MS_Dhoni_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg/500px-Wicketkeeping_kit_and_bat_of_MS_Dhoni_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg 1.5x" data-file-width="3328" data-file-height="1872"></a><figcaption>Wicket-keeping kit and bat used by Dhoni during the <a href="/wiki/2011_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2011 Cricket World Cup">2011 Cricket World Cup</a> on display at the <a href="/wiki/Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum" title="Blades of Glory Cricket Museum">Blades of Glory Cricket Museum</a></figcaption></figure>
<p>Dhoni led the Indian squad for the <a href="/wiki/2011_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2011 Cricket World Cup">2011 Cricket World Cup</a> co-hosted by India.<sup id="cite_ref-74" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-74"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>74<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> India won its second ever ODI world cup after defeating Sri Lanka in the <a href="/wiki/2011_Cricket_World_Cup_final" title="2011 Cricket World Cup final">final</a> with Dhoni being named man of the match for scoring an unbeaten 91.<sup id="cite_ref-WC_75-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-WC-75"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>75<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In December 2012, Pakistan toured India for a <a href="/wiki/Pakistani_cricket_team_in_India_in_2012%E2%80%9313" title="Pakistani cricket team in India in 2012–13">bilateral series</a> for the first time in five years and Dhoni top-scored in all the three matches of the series with a century in the first ODI at Chennai.<sup id="cite_ref-76" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-76"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>76<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led India to victory in the <a href="/wiki/2013_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2013 ICC Champions Trophy">2013 ICC Champions Trophy</a> and became the first and the only captain in international cricket to claim all <a href="/wiki/International_Cricket_Council" title="International Cricket Council">ICC</a> limited overs trophies. In the rain-shortened final against England, India won by five runs on <a href="/wiki/Duckworth%E2%80%93Lewis%E2%80%93Stern_method" title="Duckworth–Lewis–Stern method">DLS method</a> though Dhoni himself was out for a duck.<sup id="cite_ref-77" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-77"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>77<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He was also named as captain and wicketkeeper of the 'Team of the Tournament' by the ICC.<sup id="cite_ref-78" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-78"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>78<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<figure class="mw-default-size" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni_batting.JPG" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/16/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni_batting.JPG/250px-Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni_batting.JPG" decoding="async" width="250" height="125" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/16/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni_batting.JPG/500px-Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni_batting.JPG 1.5x" data-file-width="1056" data-file-height="528"></a><figcaption>Dhoni batting against South Africa during a group stage match of <a href="/wiki/2013_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2013 ICC Champions Trophy">2013 ICC Champions Trophy</a></figcaption></figure>
<p>After the Champions Trophy, India toured West Indies for a <a href="/wiki/2013_West_Indies_Tri-Series" title="2013 West Indies Tri-Series">tri-nation tournament</a> against the hosts and Sri Lanka.<sup id="cite_ref-79" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-79"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>79<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni got injured at the start of the tournament thus ruling him out for most of the tournament. He returned to play the final where he was adjudged Man of the match for scoring 45 runs off 52 balls including 16 runs in the final <a href="/wiki/Over_(cricket)" title="Over (cricket)">over</a> to take India to victory by one wicket.<sup id="cite_ref-80" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-80"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>80<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In November 2013, Dhoni became the second India batsman after <a href="/wiki/Sachin_Tendulkar" title="Sachin Tendulkar">Sachin Tendulkar</a> to aggregate more than thousand runs in ODIs against Australia.<sup id="cite_ref-81" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-81"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>81<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> India toured <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_South_Africa_in_2013%E2%80%9314" title="Indian cricket team in South Africa in 2013–14">South Africa</a> and <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_New_Zealand_in_2013%E2%80%9314" title="Indian cricket team in New Zealand in 2013–14">New Zealand</a> in the 2013–14 season. Though Dhoni scored 84 runs at an average of 48 including one half-century against South Africa and 272 runs with three consecutive 50-plus scores against New Zealand, India lost both the series.<sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-4" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni himself reached 8000 runs in ODI in the series against New Zealand.<sup id="cite_ref-82" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-82"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>82<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led India in the <a href="/wiki/2014_ICC_World_Twenty20" class="mw-redirect" title="2014 ICC World Twenty20">2014 ICC World Twenty20</a> where India finished as <a href="/wiki/Runners-up" class="mw-redirect" title="Runners-up">runners-up</a> after losing to Sri Lanka in the final.<sup id="cite_ref-83" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-83"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>83<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He was named as captain and wicket-keeper of the 'Team of the Tournament' by the ICC.<sup id="cite_ref-84" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-84"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>84<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>India won the away ODI series in England in <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_England_in_2014" title="Indian cricket team in England in 2014">2014</a> and <a href="/wiki/West_Indian_cricket_team_in_India_in_2014%E2%80%9315" title="West Indian cricket team in India in 2014–15">series</a> against West Indies in India where Dhoni scored 146 runs across the five innings he batted.<sup id="cite_ref-85" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-85"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>85<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-ODI_45-5" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ODI-45"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Test_retirement_and_2015_World_cup">Test retirement and 2015 World cup</h3></div>
<p>Dhoni played his last series during <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Australia_in_2014%E2%80%9315" title="Indian cricket team in Australia in 2014–15">India's tour of Australia</a> in December 2014. Following the third Test in <a href="/wiki/Melbourne_Cricket_Ground" title="Melbourne Cricket Ground">Melbourne</a>, Dhoni announced his retirement from the format.<sup id="cite_ref-86" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-86"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>86<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In his last test, he effected nine dismissals (eight catches and a stumping), and in the process, went past <a href="/wiki/Kumar_Sangakkara" title="Kumar Sangakkara">Kumar Sangakkara</a>'s record for most <a href="/wiki/Dismissal_(cricket)" title="Dismissal (cricket)">stumpings</a> in international cricket and also set a record for effecting the most dismissals in a match by an <a href="/wiki/List_of_India_Test_wicket-keepers" title="List of India Test wicket-keepers">Indian wicketkeeper</a> until it was broken by <a href="/wiki/Wriddhiman_Saha" title="Wriddhiman Saha">Wriddhiman Saha</a> in 2018.<sup id="cite_ref-87" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-87"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>87<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-88" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-88"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>88<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the <a href="/wiki/Carlton_Mid_Triangular_Series_in_Australia_in_2014%E2%80%9315" title="Carlton Mid Triangular Series in Australia in 2014–15">Carlton Mid triangular series</a> in Australia, India failed to win a single match with Dhoni himself managing just 70 runs from three innings at an average of 23.34.<sup id="cite_ref-89" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-89"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>89<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>During the <a href="/wiki/2015_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2015 Cricket World Cup">2015 Cricket World Cup</a>, Dhoni became the first Indian captain to win all group stage matches in a world cup.<sup id="cite_ref-90" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-90"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>90<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the match against <a href="/wiki/Zimbabwe_national_cricket_team" title="Zimbabwe national cricket team">Zimbabwe</a> at <a href="/wiki/Eden_Park" title="Eden Park">Auckland</a>, he made 85 which was the highest score by an Indian captain in New Zealand. After beating Bangladesh in the quarter finals, he became the third overall and the first non-Australian captain to win 100 ODI matches.<sup id="cite_ref-91" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-91"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>91<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> India lost to eventual champions Australia in the semi-finals with Dhoni having a good series, scoring 237 runs in six innings at an average of 59.25 and a <a href="/wiki/Strike_rate" title="Strike rate">strike rate</a> of 102.15, and thus, becoming the second Indian captain to have an average over 50 and strike rate over 100 in a particular season of the World Cup.<sup id="cite_ref-92" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-92"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>92<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-93" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-93"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>93<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Final_years_and_retirement">Final years and retirement</h3></div>
<p>Dhoni led India to victory in the <a href="/wiki/2016_Asia_Cup" title="2016 Asia Cup">2016 Asia Cup</a>, where India remained unbeaten.<sup id="cite_ref-94" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-94"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>94<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni stepped down as the captain of India in January 2017, ahead of the <a href="/wiki/English_cricket_team_in_India_in_2016%E2%80%9317" title="English cricket team in India in 2016–17">ODI series</a> at home against England.<sup id="cite_ref-95" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-95"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>95<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the second game of the series, he scored 134 off 122 balls, his tenth century in ODIs and his first in over three years.<sup id="cite_ref-96" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-96"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>96<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He was named as a wicket-keeper of the 'Team of the Tournament' at the <a href="/wiki/2017_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2017 ICC Champions Trophy">2017 ICC Champions Trophy</a> in which India finished as <a href="/wiki/Runners-up" class="mw-redirect" title="Runners-up">runners-up</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-97" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-97"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>97<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-98" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-98"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>98<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In August 2017, during the fifth and final ODI <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Sri_Lanka_in_2017" title="Indian cricket team in Sri Lanka in 2017">against Sri Lanka</a> in <a href="/wiki/R._Premadasa_Stadium" title="R. Premadasa Stadium">Colombo</a>, he became the first wicket-keeper to effect 100 stumpings in ODIs when he stumped <a href="/wiki/Akila_Dananjaya" title="Akila Dananjaya">Akila Dananjaya</a> off <a href="/wiki/Yuzvendra_Chahal" title="Yuzvendra Chahal">Yuzvendra Chahal</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-99" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-99"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>99<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He reached the milestone of effecting 400 dismissals in ODIs in February 2018, following the stumping of <a href="/wiki/Aiden_Markram" title="Aiden Markram">Aiden Markram</a> in the third ODI of the <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_South_Africa_in_2017%E2%80%9318" title="Indian cricket team in South Africa in 2017–18">South Africa tour</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-100" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-100"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>100<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Though, he had a relatively mediocre series scoring 79 runs in two innings at a strike rate of 63.20 during India's <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_England_in_2018" title="Indian cricket team in England in 2018">2018 tour of England</a>, he went past 10,000 ODI runs, becoming the fourth Indian and twelfth overall to do so.<sup id="cite_ref-101" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-101"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>101<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-102" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-102"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>102<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-103" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-103"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>103<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the <a href="/wiki/2018_Asia_Cup" title="2018 Asia Cup">2018 Asia Cup</a> title winning campaign, he scored just 77 runs in four innings at an average of 19.25.<sup id="cite_ref-104" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-104"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>104<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-105" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-105"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>105<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> While captaining in the group stage match against <a href="/wiki/Afghanistan_cricket_team" class="mw-redirect" title="Afghanistan cricket team">Afghanistan</a> due to regular captain <a href="/wiki/Rohit_Sharma" title="Rohit Sharma">Rohit Sharma</a> being unavailable, Dhoni became the first cricketer to lead India 200 times in ODIs.<sup id="cite_ref-106" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-106"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>106<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni aggregated 50 runs from three innings in the <a href="/wiki/West_Indian_cricket_team_in_India_in_2018%E2%80%9319" title="West Indian cricket team in India in 2018–19">home series against West Indies</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-107" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-107"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>107<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-108" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-108"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>108<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the series, he effected the fastest stumping in the history of cricket, clocked at 0.08 seconds, when dismissing <a href="/wiki/Keemo_Paul" title="Keemo Paul">Keemo Paul</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-109" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-109"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>109<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-110" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-110"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>110<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni was not selected for the T20I squad for the series that followed and the <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team_in_Australia_in_2018%E2%80%9319" title="Indian cricket team in Australia in 2018–19">Australia tour</a> later that season.<sup id="cite_ref-111" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-111"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>111<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> However, he was included in the squad for the ODI series in Australia.<sup id="cite_ref-112" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-112"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>112<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In the three-match series, Dhoni scored half-centuries in all three games with the latter two resulting in wins, helping India secure a 2–1 series victory, their first in a bilateral series on Australian soil and was named player of the series while also becoming the fourth Indian to score more than 1,000 ODI runs in Australia.<sup id="cite_ref-113" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-113"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>113<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In April 2019, he was named in <a href="/wiki/2019_Cricket_World_Cup_squads#India" title="2019 Cricket World Cup squads">India's squad</a> for the <a href="/wiki/2019_Cricket_World_Cup" title="2019 Cricket World Cup">2019 Cricket World Cup</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-114" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-114"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>114<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-115" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-115"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>115<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> On 9 July 2019, Dhoni played in his 350th and final ODI in the semi-final loss against <a href="/wiki/New_Zealand_national_cricket_team" title="New Zealand national cricket team">New Zealand.</a><sup id="cite_ref-116" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-116"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>116<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> On 15 August 2020, Dhoni formally announced his retirement from international cricket.<sup id="cite_ref-117" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-117"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>117<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Post-international_retirement">Post-international retirement</h3></div>
<p>On 8 September 2021, Dhoni was appointed as the mentor of the Indian team for the <a href="/wiki/2021_Men%27s_T20_World_Cup" title="2021 Men's T20 World Cup">2021 T20 World Cup</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-118" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-118"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>118<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-119" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-119"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>119<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-120" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-120"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>120<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Domestic_career">Domestic career</h2></div>
<p>Dhoni played for Bihar state cricket team since 1999 before representing Jharkhand later. He has also played for Rajasthan Cricket Association President's XI, East zone and <a href="/wiki/Rest_of_India_cricket_team" title="Rest of India cricket team">Rest of India</a> in domestic cricket.<sup id="cite_ref-Cric_121-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Cric-121"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In <a href="/wiki/BCCI_Corporate_Trophy" title="BCCI Corporate Trophy">BCCI Corporate trophy</a>, he played for <a href="/wiki/Air_India" title="Air India">Air India</a> until his resignation from the company in 2013.<sup id="cite_ref-122" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-122"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>122<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In February 2005, Dhoni played for India seniors in <a href="/wiki/NKP_Salve_Challenger_Trophy" title="NKP Salve Challenger Trophy">Challenger trophy</a>, where he scored 102 against India B.<sup id="cite_ref-123" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-123"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>123<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Jharkhand_State_Cricket_Association" title="Jharkhand State Cricket Association">Jharkhand State Cricket Association</a> (JSCA) appointed him captain of the Jharkhand team in February 2017 for the <a href="/wiki/2017-18_Vijay_Hazare_Trophy" class="mw-redirect" title="2017-18 Vijay Hazare Trophy">2017-18 Vijay Hazare trophy</a> and on 25 February 2017, he scored his first ever domestic list-A century against <a href="/wiki/Chhattisgarh_cricket_team" title="Chhattisgarh cricket team">Chhattisgarh</a> and led the team to the quarter final where Jharkhand lost against <a href="/wiki/Delhi_cricket_team" title="Delhi cricket team">Delhi</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-124" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-124"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>124<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-125" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-125"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>125<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>

<tr>

</th></tr>
