<td style="text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;color:inherit;background-color:silver;color:inherit;"><b>Runner-up</b></td>
<td style="text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;"><span class="nowrap"><a href="/wiki/2017_Indian_Premier_League" title="2017 Indian Premier League">2017</a></span></td>
<td style="text-align:center;vertical-align:middle;">
</td></tr></tbody></table>
<figure class="mw-default-size mw-halign-left" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg/250px-MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg" decoding="async" width="200" height="150" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg/330px-MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg/500px-MS_Dhoni_in_2011.jpg 2x" data-file-width="585" data-file-height="439"></a><figcaption>Dhoni with Chennai Super Kings in 2011</figcaption></figure>
<p>Dhoni was acquired by <a href="/wiki/Chennai_Super_Kings" title="Chennai Super Kings">Chennai Super Kings</a> (CSK) for US$1.5 million during the <a href="/wiki/List_of_2008_Indian_Premier_League_auctions_and_personnel_signings" title="List of 2008 Indian Premier League auctions and personnel signings">auction</a> for the <a href="/wiki/2008_Indian_Premier_League" title="2008 Indian Premier League">inaugural season</a> of the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Premier_League" title="Indian Premier League">Indian Premier League</a> (IPL). He was the most expensive signing in the auction.<sup id="cite_ref-126" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-126"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>126<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored 414 runs to lead CSK to the finals in the first season of IPL.<sup id="cite_ref-IPL_127-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-IPL-127"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>127<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Under his captaincy, CSK won the <a href="/wiki/2010_Indian_Premier_League" title="2010 Indian Premier League">2010 edition</a> and qualified for <a href="/wiki/Champions_League_Twenty20" title="Champions League Twenty20">Champions League Twenty20</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-128" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-128"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>128<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Chennai won the <a href="/wiki/2010_Champions_League_Twenty20" title="2010 Champions League Twenty20">2010 Champions league</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-129" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-129"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>129<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored 392 runs and led CSK to its second consecutive IPL title in <a href="/wiki/2011_Indian_Premier_League" title="2011 Indian Premier League">2011</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-CSK_130-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CSK-130"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>130<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led CSK to its second Champions League Twenty20 title in <a href="/wiki/2014_Champions_League_Twenty20" title="2014 Champions League Twenty20">2014</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-131" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-131"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>131<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni scored 2987 runs from 129 matches in the IPL across the first eight seasons for the Super Kings.<sup id="cite_ref-IPL_127-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-IPL-127"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>127<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Following the two-year suspension of Chennai Super Kings and <a href="/wiki/Rajasthan_Royals" title="Rajasthan Royals">Rajasthan Royals</a>, two new franchises <a href="/wiki/Rising_Pune_Supergiants" class="mw-redirect" title="Rising Pune Supergiants">Rising Pune Supergiants</a> and <a href="/wiki/Gujarat_Lions" title="Gujarat Lions">Gujarat Lions</a> were established for the <a href="/wiki/2016_Indian_Premier_League" title="2016 Indian Premier League">2016 Indian Premier League</a> season. Supergiants picked Dhoni as one of their five <a href="/wiki/List_of_2016_Indian_Premier_League_personnel_changes" title="List of 2016 Indian Premier League personnel changes">draft</a> picks on 15 December 2015 for <span class="nowrap"><span style="white-space: nowrap">₹</span>125 million</span> (US$1.5 million).<sup id="cite_ref-amol_132-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-amol-132"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>132<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He scored 574 runs in 30 matches across two seasons for the Supergiants.<sup id="cite_ref-IPL_127-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-IPL-127"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>127<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni <a href="/wiki/List_of_2018_Indian_Premier_League_personnel_changes" title="List of 2018 Indian Premier League personnel changes">returned</a> to CSK for the <a href="/wiki/2018_Indian_Premier_League" title="2018 Indian Premier League">2018 season</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-133" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-133"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>133<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He scored 455 runs and led his team to its third IPL title.<sup id="cite_ref-134" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-134"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>134<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led CSK to the title again in <a href="/wiki/2021_Indian_Premier_League" title="2021 Indian Premier League">2021</a> and was retained for <span class="nowrap"><span style="white-space: nowrap">₹</span>12 crore</span> (US$1.4 million) before the <a href="/wiki/List_of_2022_Indian_Premier_League_personnel_changes" title="List of 2022 Indian Premier League personnel changes">auction</a> for the 2022 season.<sup id="cite_ref-135" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-135"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>135<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni stepped down from captaincy ahead of that season and <a href="/wiki/Ravindra_Jadeja" title="Ravindra Jadeja">Ravindra Jadeja</a> was appointed the new captain.<sup id="cite_ref-136" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-136"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>136<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-137" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-137"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>137<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> However, a month later, Jadeja handed over the captaincy back to Dhoni in the middle of the season.<sup id="cite_ref-138" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-138"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>138<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni led the franchise to victory again the <a href="/wiki/2023_Indian_Premier_League" title="2023 Indian Premier League">following season</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-CSK_130-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CSK-130"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>130<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Under his captaincy, CSK became the most successful IPL franchise with five title wins and ten final appearances.<sup id="cite_ref-139" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-139"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>139<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni became the first player to play 200 T20 matches for CSK and holds the record for most appearances in the IPL.<sup id="cite_ref-140" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-140"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>140<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Ahead of the <a href="/wiki/2024_Indian_Premier_League" title="2024 Indian Premier League">2024 season</a>, Dhoni handed over captaincy to <a href="/wiki/Ruturaj_Gaikwad" title="Ruturaj Gaikwad">Ruturaj Gaikwad</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-141" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-141"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>141<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He marked his 250th appearance for CSK in that season, in a league stage match against <a href="/wiki/Mumbai_Indians" title="Mumbai Indians">Mumbai Indians</a> on 14 April 2024, helping his team secure a 20-run win, following his unbeaten four-ball 20.<sup id="cite_ref-142" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-142"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>142<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Outside_cricket">Outside cricket</h2></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:The_Chief_of_Army_Staff,_Gen._V.K._Singh_pipping_in_the_Rank_of_Hon._Lt._Col._to_Indian_Skipper_M.S._Dhoni,_in_New_Delhi_on_November_01,_2011.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bb/The_Chief_of_Army_Staff%2C_Gen._V.K._Singh_pipping_in_the_Rank_of_Hon._Lt._Col._to_Indian_Skipper_M.S._Dhoni%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_November_01%2C_2011.jpg/250px-The_Chief_of_Army_Staff%2C_Gen._V.K._Singh_pipping_in_the_Rank_of_Hon._Lt._Col._to_Indian_Skipper_M.S._Dhoni%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_November_01%2C_2011.jpg" decoding="async" width="250" height="156" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bb/The_Chief_of_Army_Staff%2C_Gen._V.K._Singh_pipping_in_the_Rank_of_Hon._Lt._Col._to_Indian_Skipper_M.S._Dhoni%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_November_01%2C_2011.jpg/500px-The_Chief_of_Army_Staff%2C_Gen._V.K._Singh_pipping_in_the_Rank_of_Hon._Lt._Col._to_Indian_Skipper_M.S._Dhoni%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_November_01%2C_2011.jpg 1.5x" data-file-width="2200" data-file-height="1375"></a><figcaption><a href="/wiki/Chief_of_Army_Staff" class="mw-redirect" title="Chief of Army Staff">Chief of Army Staff</a> <a href="/wiki/General" class="mw-redirect" title="General">General</a> <a href="/wiki/V._K._Singh" title="V. K. Singh">V. K. Singh</a> bestowing the <a href="/wiki/Honorary_rank" class="mw-redirect" title="Honorary rank">honorary rank</a> of <a href="/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel" title="Lieutenant colonel">Lieutenant colonel</a> to Dhoni</figcaption></figure>
<p>Dhoni holds an <a href="/wiki/Honorary_rank" class="mw-redirect" title="Honorary rank">honorary rank</a> of <a href="/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel" title="Lieutenant colonel">Lieutenant colonel</a> in the <a href="/wiki/Parachute_Regiment_(India)" title="Parachute Regiment (India)">Parachute Regiment</a> of the <a href="/wiki/Territorial_Army_(India)" title="Territorial Army (India)">Indian Territorial Army</a> (106 Para TA battalion).<sup id="cite_ref-143" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-143"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>143<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The honorary rank was presented to him by the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Army" title="Indian Army">Indian Army</a> in 2011 for his service to the nation as a cricketer.<sup id="cite_ref-Col_144-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Col-144"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>144<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> After completing five parachute training jumps from Indian Army aircraft in the Agra training camp, he became a <a href="/wiki/Paratrooper" title="Paratrooper">qualified paratrooper</a> in 2015.<sup id="cite_ref-145" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-145"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>145<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In August 2019, he completed a two-week stint with the Territorial Army in <a href="/wiki/Jammu_and_Kashmir_(state)" title="Jammu and Kashmir (state)">Jammu and Kashmir</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-146" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-146"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>146<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> While spending a day with the <a href="/wiki/Parachute_Regiment_(India)" title="Parachute Regiment (India)">parachute regiment</a> in Ranchi, Dhoni said that he wanted to become a soldier and not a cricketer, "Since childhood I wanted to join the Army. Seeing the soldiers, I thought that one day I'll be the same".<sup id="cite_ref-147" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-147"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>147<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni holds the post of vice-president in <a href="/wiki/India_Cements" title="India Cements">India Cements Ltd</a>, the company owned by former BCCI president <a href="/wiki/N._Srinivasan" title="N. Srinivasan">N. Srinivasan</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-148" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-148"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>148<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-149" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-149"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>149<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni is a co-owner of <a href="/wiki/Chennai" title="Chennai">Chennai</a>-based football club <a href="/wiki/Chennaiyin_FC" title="Chennaiyin FC">Chennaiyin FC</a>, a franchise of the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Super_League" title="Indian Super League">Indian Super League</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-150" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-150"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>150<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He also co-owns Ranchi-based <a href="/wiki/Field_hockey" title="Field hockey">hockey</a> club <a href="/wiki/Ranchi_Rays" title="Ranchi Rays">Ranchi Rays</a>, a franchise of the <a href="/wiki/Hockey_India_League" title="Hockey India League">Hockey India League</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-151" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-151"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>151<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In February 2016, Dhoni launched lifestyle brand <a href="/wiki/SEVEN_(brand)" class="mw-redirect" title="SEVEN (brand)">SEVEN</a> which he co-owns while also serving as its brand ambassador.<sup id="cite_ref-152" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-152"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>152<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In 2019, Dhoni invested in vehicle re-seller <a href="/wiki/CARS24" title="CARS24">CARS24</a> and simultaneously became brand ambassador of the company.<sup id="cite_ref-153" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-153"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>153<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> On 11 October 2022, Dhoni invested in Shaka Harry, a plant-based protein company.<sup id="cite_ref-154" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-154"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>154<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-155" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-155"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>155<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<figure class="mw-default-size" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:MS_Dhoni%27s_jersey_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum,_Pune.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/MS_Dhoni%27s_jersey_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg/250px-MS_Dhoni%27s_jersey_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg" decoding="async" width="250" height="141" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/MS_Dhoni%27s_jersey_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg/500px-MS_Dhoni%27s_jersey_at_Blades_of_Glory_Cricket_Museum%2C_Pune.jpg 1.5x" data-file-width="3328" data-file-height="1872"></a><figcaption>The No.7 jersey of the <a href="/wiki/Indian_cricket_team" class="mw-redirect" title="Indian cricket team">Indian cricket team</a> made popular by Dhoni</figcaption></figure>
<p><span class="anchor" id="Dhoni_Entertainment"></span>In 2019, Dhoni Entertainment entered into a long-term business agreement with <a href="/wiki/Banijay_Group" class="mw-redirect" title="Banijay Group">Banijay Asia</a> to produce content across various genres.<sup id="cite_ref-156" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-156"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>156<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The first show developed by the company was a documentary web series titled <i><a href="/wiki/Roar_of_The_Lion_(documentary)" class="mw-redirect" title="Roar of The Lion (documentary)">Roar of The Lion</a></i> for <a href="/wiki/Disney%2B_Hotstar" title="Disney+ Hotstar">Disney+ Hotstar</a> in 2019 which dealt with the ban of Chennai Super Kings from the Indian Premier League in 2016 and their return to win the title in 2018.<sup id="cite_ref-RoR_157-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-RoR-157"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>157<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The production house produced a <a href="/wiki/Tamil_language" title="Tamil language">Tamil language</a> romantic drama <i><a href="/wiki/LGM_(film)" class="mw-redirect" title="LGM (film)">LGM</a></i> which was released on 28 July 2023.<sup id="cite_ref-158" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-158"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>158<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-159" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-159"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>159<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-160" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-160"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>160<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni performed a special cameo appearance in <a href="/wiki/Vijay_(actor)" title="Vijay (actor)">Vijay</a>'s film <i><a href="/wiki/The_Greatest_of_All_Time" title="The Greatest of All Time">G.O.A.T</a></i> that was released on 5 September 2024.<sup id="cite_ref-161" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-161"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>161<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>Dhoni is one of the most popular cricketers in the world.<sup id="cite_ref-162" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-162"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>162<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> As of 2022<sup class="plainlinks noexcerpt noprint asof-tag update" style="display:none;"><a class="external text" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=MS_Dhoni&action=edit">[update]</a></sup>, Dhoni has more than 75 million followers on social media platforms and his brand value was projected to be $80.3 million by <a href="/wiki/Kroll_Inc." title="Kroll Inc.">Duff and Phelps</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-163" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-163"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>163<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni's popularity is often compared to Sachin Tendulkar, the highest run scorer in international cricket.<sup id="cite_ref-164" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-164"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>164<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-165" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-165"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>165<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The No.7 jersey was made popular by Dhoni and was retired by the BCCI in 2023 as a honour to him.<sup id="cite_ref-166" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-166"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>166<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni has developed a special connection with the city of <a href="/wiki/Chennai" title="Chennai">Chennai</a> whose IPL franchise he represents and the fans often call him 'Thala' meaning leader in Tamil.<sup id="cite_ref-167" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-167"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>167<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-168" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-168"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>168<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Playing_style">Playing style</h2></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a href="/wiki/File:MS_Dhoni_2.jpg" class="mw-file-description"><img src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3b/MS_Dhoni_2.jpg/250px-MS_Dhoni_2.jpg" decoding="async" width="200" height="241" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3b/MS_Dhoni_2.jpg/330px-MS_Dhoni_2.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3b/MS_Dhoni_2.jpg/500px-MS_Dhoni_2.jpg 2x" data-file-width="1085" data-file-height="1306"></a><figcaption>Dhoni in 2008</figcaption></figure>
<p>Dhoni is a right-handed <a href="/wiki/Batsman" class="mw-redirect" title="Batsman">batsman</a> and <a href="/wiki/Wicket-keeper" title="Wicket-keeper">wicket-keeper</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Cric_121-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Cric-121"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni is an unorthodox batsman who deviates from conventional coaching manuals to showcase his batting technique.<sup id="cite_ref-Uno_169-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Uno-169"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>169<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni displays a proclivity for hitting <a href="/wiki/Full_length_(bowling)" class="mw-redirect" title="Full length (bowling)">full-length</a> deliveries towards the <a href="/wiki/Fielding_(cricket)" title="Fielding (cricket)">Long-on</a>, <a href="/wiki/Long_off" class="mw-redirect" title="Long off">Long-off</a>, and <a href="/wiki/Midwicket" class="mw-redirect" title="Midwicket">Midwicket regions</a>, rather than the conventional Cover region.<sup id="cite_ref-Uno_169-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Uno-169"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>169<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He plays <a href="/wiki/Pull_shot" class="mw-redirect" title="Pull shot">pull shots</a> and hook shots off <a href="/wiki/Short_pitched_bowling" class="mw-redirect" title="Short pitched bowling">short-pitched</a> deliveries often putting pressure on the bowler to adjust their line and length accordingly. Dhoni holds the bat with a firm grip at the bottom of the handle, striking the ball with force and precision to clear the boundary.<sup id="cite_ref-170" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-170"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>170<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He is a powerful hitter of the ball and is one of the fastest men in running between the wickets.<sup id="cite_ref-171" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-171"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>171<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He plays the <a href="/wiki/Helicopter_shot" title="Helicopter shot">helicopter shot</a> technique, taught to him by a fellow player and childhood friend <a href="/wiki/Santosh_Lal" title="Santosh Lal">Santosh Lal</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-172" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-172"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>172<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-173" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-173"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>173<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> As a batsman, he is recognised for his finishing skills in high-pressure situations.<sup id="cite_ref-174" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-174"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>174<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p><p>As a wicket-keeper, he has been praised for his fast reflexes behind the <a href="/wiki/Stump_(cricket)" title="Stump (cricket)">stumps</a> while also being criticised for the lack of good technique.<sup id="cite_ref-175" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-175"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>175<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-176" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-176"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>176<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He is known for his unorthodox captaincy, approachability and has earned a reputation of being a successful leader.<sup id="cite_ref-177" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-177"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>177<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-178" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-178"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>178<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni is also known for his cool-headed demeanor on the field which has earned him the monicker "Captain cool".<sup id="cite_ref-179" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-179"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>179<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Personal_life">Personal life</h2></div>
<p>Dhoni married Sakshi Singh Rawat on 4 July 2010 in <a href="/wiki/Dehradun" title="Dehradun">Dehradun</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-180" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-180"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>180<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-181" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-181"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>181<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni and his wife have a daughter, Ziva Dhoni who was born on 6 February 2015.<sup id="cite_ref-182" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-182"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>182<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-183" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-183"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>183<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He lives in his farmhouse outside Ranchi.<sup id="cite_ref-184" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-184"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>184<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni is an automotive enthusiast and owns a number of bikes and cars in his collection.<sup id="cite_ref-185" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-185"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>185<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Career_statistics">Career statistics</h2></div>
<p>Dhoni has scored 4876 runs in Test matches at an average of 38 and 10773 runs at an average of above 50 in ODIs. He has scored 16 centuries and 108 fifties in his international career.<sup id="cite_ref-Cric_121-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Cric-121"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Dhoni has one of the highest averages in ODI cricket.<sup id="cite_ref-186" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-186"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>186<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He has scored more than 7400 runs across 390 T20 matches.<sup id="cite_ref-Cric_121-3" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Cric-121"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> He has taken 634 catches and effected 195 stumpings in his international career, making him one of the most prolific wicket-keepers of all time.<sup id="cite_ref-WK_187-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-WK-187"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>187<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>

<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Captaincy">Captaincy</h3></div>
<p>In 2007, Dhoni was appointed <a href="/wiki/Indian_national_cricket_captains" class="mw-redirect" title="Indian national cricket captains">captain of the Indian team</a> and served as the captain of all formats from 2008. He captained the Indian team in 332 matches including 200 ODIs and was one of the most prolific and successful captains of all time.<sup id="cite_ref-CT_190-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CT-190"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>190<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-CT2_191-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CT2-191"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>191<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>

<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Records_and_achievements">Records and achievements</h2></div>
<dl><dt>Tests</dt></dl>
<ul><li>Most runs by an Indian wicket-keeper (4876)<sup id="cite_ref-193" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-193"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>193<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most number of sixes by an Indian captain (51)<sup id="cite_ref-194" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-194"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>194<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most dismissals by an Indian and fifth most by any wicket-keeper (294)<sup id="cite_ref-wicket-keeper_195-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-wicket-keeper-195"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>195<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>
<dl><dt>ODIs</dt></dl>
<ul><li>Most wins by an Indian captain and second most overall (110)<sup id="cite_ref-196" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-196"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>196<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Second most runs as captain (6641)<sup id="cite_ref-197" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-197"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>197<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Third most number of matches as captain (200)<sup id="cite_ref-CT_190-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CT-190"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>190<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>First player to pass 10,000 runs with an average of over 50<sup id="cite_ref-198" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-198"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>198<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most not-outs (84)<sup id="cite_ref-199" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-199"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>199<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Highest score by a wicket-keeper (183<a href="/wiki/Not_out" title="Not out">*</a>)<sup id="cite_ref-200" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-200"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>200<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Highest eighth wicket partnership for India (100<a href="/wiki/Not_out" title="Not out">*</a> with <a href="/wiki/Bhuvneshwar_Kumar" title="Bhuvneshwar Kumar">Bhuvneshwar Kumar</a>)<sup id="cite_ref-201" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-201"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>201<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most dismissals in an innings (6) and career (432) by an Indian wicket-keeper<sup id="cite_ref-202" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-202"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>202<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most stumpings by any wicket-keeper (123)<sup id="cite_ref-203" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-203"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>203<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-204" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-204"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>204<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>
<dl><dt>T20Is</dt></dl>
<ul><li>Second most matches as captain (72)<sup id="cite_ref-CT2_191-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-CT2-191"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>191<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most T20I innings (76) and runs (1,153) before scoring a fifty<sup id="cite_ref-205" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-205"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>205<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-206" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-206"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>206<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most stumpings as wicket-keeper (34)<sup id="cite_ref-207" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-207"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>207<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most catches as wicket keeper in a T20I innings (5)<sup id="cite_ref-208" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-208"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>208<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>
<dl><dt>Combined</dt></dl>
<ul><li>Most international matches as captain (332)<sup id="cite_ref-209" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-209"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>209<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Most stumpings (195) and only wicket-keeper to make 150 stumpings<sup id="cite_ref-210" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-210"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>210<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-211" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-211"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>211<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Third most dismissals as a wicket-keeper (829)<sup id="cite_ref-WK_187-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-WK-187"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>187<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Sixth most sixes in career (359)<sup id="cite_ref-212" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-212"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>212<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>

<ul><li><a href="/wiki/T20_World_Cup" title="T20 World Cup">T20 World Cup</a>: <a href="/wiki/2007_ICC_World_T20" class="mw-redirect" title="2007 ICC World T20">2007</a><sup id="cite_ref-213" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-213"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>213<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>

<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="ICC Champions Trophy">ICC Champions Trophy</a>: <a href="/wiki/2013_ICC_Champions_Trophy" title="2013 ICC Champions Trophy">2013</a><sup id="cite_ref-218" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-218"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>218<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-219" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-219"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>219<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>

<ul><li><a href="/wiki/MTV_Youth_Icon_of_the_Year" title="MTV Youth Icon of the Year">MTV Youth Icon of the Year</a>: 2006<sup id="cite_ref-228" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-228"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>228<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-229" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-229"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>229<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Men%27s_ODI_Team_of_the_Year" title="ICC Men's ODI Team of the Year">ICC Men's ODI Team of the Year</a>: 2006, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014 (captain in 2009, 2011–2014)<sup id="cite_ref-230" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-230"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>230<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-231" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-231"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>231<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Men%27s_Test_Team_of_the_Year" title="ICC Men's Test Team of the Year">ICC Men's Test Team of the Year</a>: 2009, 2010, 2013 (captain in 2009, 2010)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Khel_Ratna_Award" title="Khel Ratna Award">Major Dhyanchand Khel Ratna award</a>: 2008<sup id="cite_ref-232" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-232"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>232<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Awards#ODI_Cricketer_Of_The_Year" title="ICC Awards">ICC ODI Cricketer of the Year</a>: 2008, 2009<sup id="cite_ref-233" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-233"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>233<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-234" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-234"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>234<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Padma_Shri" title="Padma Shri">Padma Shri</a>: 2009<sup id="cite_ref-235" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-235"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>235<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Honorary <a href="/wiki/Lieutenant_colonel" title="Lieutenant colonel">Lieutenant Colonel</a>, <a href="/wiki/Parachute_Regiment_(India)" title="Parachute Regiment (India)">Parachute Regiment</a> of the <a href="/wiki/Territorial_Army_(India)" title="Territorial Army (India)">Indian Territorial Army</a>: 2011<sup id="cite_ref-Col_144-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Col-144"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>144<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/CNN-News18_Indian_of_the_Year" title="CNN-News18 Indian of the Year">CNN-News18 Indian of the Year</a>: 2011<sup id="cite_ref-236" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-236"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>236<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Castrol_Awards_for_Cricketing_Excellence" title="Castrol Awards for Cricketing Excellence">Castrol Indian Cricketer of the Year</a>: 2011<sup id="cite_ref-237" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-237"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>237<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-238" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-238"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>238<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>

<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Awards_of_the_Decade" title="ICC Awards of the Decade">ICC Men's T20I team of the decade</a>: 2011–2020 (captain and wicket-keeper)<sup id="cite_ref-ICCD_241-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-ICCD-241"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>241<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Awards_of_the_Decade" title="ICC Awards of the Decade">ICC Spirit of cricket award of the decade</a>: 2011–2020<sup id="cite_ref-242" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-242"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>242<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-243" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-243"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>243<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/ICC_Cricket_Hall_of_Fame" title="ICC Cricket Hall of Fame">ICC Cricket Hall of Fame</a><sup id="cite_ref-244" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-244"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>244<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>:2025</li></ul>
<dl><dt>Others</dt></dl>
<p>Dhoni was awarded an honorary doctorate by <a href="/wiki/De_Montfort_University" title="De Montfort University">De Montfort University</a> in 2011.<sup id="cite_ref-245" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-245"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>245<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-246" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-246"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>246<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In 2019, Jharkhand Cricket Association named the <a href="/wiki/JSCA_International_Stadium_Complex" title="JSCA International Stadium Complex">JSCA stadium</a>'s south stand after Dhoni.<sup id="cite_ref-247" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-247"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>247<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> In 2023, the <a href="/wiki/Mumbai_Cricket_Association" title="Mumbai Cricket Association">Mumbai Cricket Association</a> (MCA) decided to honour him by dedicating seats (J282–J286) at the <a href="/wiki/Wankhede_Stadium" title="Wankhede Stadium">Wankhede Stadium</a> where he hit the winning shot in <a href="/wiki/2011_Cricket_World_Cup_final" title="2011 Cricket World Cup final">2011 World Cup final</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-248" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-248"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>248<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="In_popular_culture">In popular culture</h2></div>
<ul><li>A film based on Dhoni's life from his childhood to the 2011 world cup win, titled <i><a href="/wiki/M.S._Dhoni:_The_Untold_Story" title="M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story">M.S. Dhoni: The Untold Story</a></i>, with <a href="/wiki/Sushant_Singh_Rajput" title="Sushant Singh Rajput">Sushant Singh Rajput</a> in the lead role was released on 29 September 2016.<sup id="cite_ref-249" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-249"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>249<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><i>The Dhoni Touch: unravelling the enigma that is Mahendra Singh Dhoni</i>, a book by Bharat Sundaresan.<sup id="cite_ref-250" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-250"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>250<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Dhoni_(film)" title="Dhoni (film)">Dhoni</a> (2012), a Tamil feature film, directed and produced by <a href="/wiki/Prakash_Raj" title="Prakash Raj">Prakash Raj</a> where the plot illustrates the conflicting interests of a father and his son with the father wanting his son to study <a href="/wiki/Master_of_Business_Administration" title="Master of Business Administration">MBA</a> but his son wanting to become a cricketer like Dhoni.<sup id="cite_ref-251" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-251"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>251<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>
